concerns.text: Task T015 (compute rsFC and Global Efficiency) is listed as a parallel task [P] alongside T014 (parcellation). However, T015 requires the weighted adjacency matrix output from T014 to compute Global Efficiency. T015 cannot run in parallel with T014; it must be a consumer task dependent on T014's completion.

Task T026 (Compute z-scores at three density thresholds) is marked [P] and listed after T025. However, T026 depends on the output of T022 (enumeration) and T023 (null model generation). If T022/T023 are parallelized per subject, T026 must wait for their completion. The current flat list implies T026 could start before T022/T023 finish, violating producer-consumer ordering.

Task T031 (partial correlations) and T032 (FDR correction) are marked [P]. T032 consumes the raw p-values generated by T031. T032 cannot run in parallel with T031; it must strictly follow T031. Marking T032 as [P] suggests it can run concurrently with T031, which is a logical impossibility.

Task T016 (graceful handling for missing subjects) is listed as a separate task. Logically, this logic is embedded within T013 (download) and T014 (preprocess). Listing it as a standalone parallel task [P] is semantically confusing; it should be a sub-task or integrated into the description of T013/T014 to avoid implying a separate execution step.
